gas_config_change: Switched from AKASH_GAS_PRICES=0.5uakt + GAS_ADJUSTMENT=2 (xion_ops defaults) to documented 0.025uakt + 1.5. Effect: close-deployment tx cost dropped from 268652-668623 uakt to 3895-10066 uakt (30x-170x reduction).
context: Smoke retry against KW-FLOOR-DEPLOY-001. Three attempts in this session. Goal: isolate whether manifest-refusal pattern is SDL-shaped, provider-shaped, or network-shaped. Used minimal nginx /health SDL.

attempts.detail: Manifest accepted (new — audited filter delivered a working bidder). Health-smoke HTTP 503. Auto-close succeeded at OLD gas (268652 uakt).
'submit manifest to some providers has been failed' — same refusal string as 2026-05-06 batch, from a different audited provider. Audited filter is necessary but not sufficient.
Manifest accepted. Health-smoke '<urlopen error [Errno 111] Connection refused>'. Auto-close succeeded at NEW gas (10066 uakt).

findings: Audited-provider signedBy filter consistently delivers manifest-accepting bidders. The 2026-05-06 'send_manifest_failed' pattern is NOT inevitable with audited filter — attempt 1 and 3 today both got past send-manifest.

Audited providers' POST-lease ingress is the new failure surface: HTTP 503 (attempt 1) and 'Connection refused' (attempt 3) on a trivial nginx /health image. Two of three audited bidders failed the health-smoke step.

uakt-gas mispricing was a major self-inflicted blocker. xion_ops defaults (0.5uakt / adj 2) overprice tx fees ~20x vs Akash docs (0.025uakt / adj 1.5). Result: an unsuccessful deploy used to bleed ~270k uakt of gas per round; now it bleeds ~10k.

Even with all fixes, audited-provider ingress reliability is the bottleneck. Akash community feedback (akash-network/support#180) suggests provider economics under BME (launched 2026-03-23) are stressed; small providers may be deferring infrastructure maintenance, producing ingress-layer failures.
Akash announced Cosmos chain deprecation October 2025; RFP for new chain (Solana frontrunner) is open. akashnet-2 will continue to operate but has finite shelf life.
decision_implications_for_KW-FLOOR-DEPLO: Akash IS a viable decentralized-option proof for the genesis floor narrative — manifest-acceptance step now works with audited+excluded config.

Akash is NOT currently a reliable PRIMARY GPU floor for production traffic — three audited providers in a row had ingress problems on a smoke probe. Closure-grade probation would require an audited subset known to have working ingress, not a blind audited filter.
Chutes / Bittensor SN64 should stay as the primary GPU path. Akash as evidence-of-option only. Re-evaluate after Akash chain migration decision.
operational_artefacts_persisted: infra/akash/relay-smoke-minimal.yaml — signedBy + host filter added (commit pending)
next_recommended: Set AKASH_GAS_PRICES=0.025uakt and AKASH_GAS_ADJUSTMENT=1.5 as defaults in scripts/demo-minimal-akash-deploy.sh (or update xion_ops/services/akash.py defaults). Do not attempt closure-grade Akash GPU until a known-good audited provider with verified ingress is identified.
